body:
Fixes #1

## Summary

- Replaces the Rust template sum example with the `formal-ai` crate, library exports, and `formal-ai` CLI.
- Adds a deterministic symbolic engine for greetings and Rust hello-world prompts with inspectable evidence links and Links Notation exports.
- Adds OpenAI-shaped Chat Completions and Responses DTOs plus a small HTTP server for `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/responses`, `/v1/models`, and `/health`.
- Adds Docker packaging, a GitHub Pages React demo, and a Rust-generated WebAssembly worker for the demo.
- Documents the issue #1 requirement matrix and case-study research under `docs/`.

## Reproduction

Before implementation, the repository still exposed only the template `example-sum-package-name::sum` API. A minimum reproducing unit test importing `formal_ai::{FormalAiEngine, create_chat_completion, create_response}` failed because none of the requested formal-AI APIs existed.

The new regression coverage verifies:

- `Hi` and `Hello` produce `Hi, how may I help you?`
- `Write me hello world program in Rust` produces a Rust code block
- Chat Completions and Responses JSON shapes are available
- Links Notation exports decode through `lino-objects-codec`
- The HTTP route handler and CLI produce the expected responses

## Demo

![formal-ai demo](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-1-040a21d753ca/docs/screenshots/formal-ai-demo.png?raw=true)

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cargo test`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/detect-code-changes.rs` with PR-like env
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs` with PR-like env
- `rust-script scripts/check-version-modification.rs` with PR-like env
- `sh docs/demo/wasm-worker/build.sh`
- `cargo package --allow-dirty`
- Browser verification with Playwright MCP against `docs/demo`: loaded the WASM worker and returned the Rust hello-world response

Docker build was not run locally because Docker is not installed in the prepared environment.

## Scope Notes

This PR is a proof of concept, not a complete universal formal reasoner. It establishes the compatible API surfaces, deterministic symbolic behavior, link-native representation, CLI/server packaging, and demo path so follow-up PRs can add larger datasets, probabilistic/formal reasoning components, and native desktop packaging behind the same contract.

comments.body:
## Working session summary

Implemented and pushed the formal-AI proof of concept to PR 2:

https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/2

What changed:
- Replaced the template sum crate with `formal-ai`.
- Added symbolic engine, CLI, HTTP server, OpenAI-shaped Chat Completions and Responses APIs.
- Added Links Notation export through `lino-objects-codec`.
- Added Dockerfile, GitHub Pages React demo, Rust-generated WASM worker, screenshot, docs, case study, and changelog fragment.
- Added unit/integration coverage for the engine, API shapes, server handler, CLI, and Links Notation export.

Verification:
- Local checks passed: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo test`, `cargo test --doc`, `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`, PR gate scripts, WASM build, `cargo package`.
- Playwright verified the demo loads the WASM worker and returns the Rust hello-world answer.
- GitHub Actions passed for commit `bdc9bbc` on run `25731903515`.
- PR is open, ready for review, and working tree is clean.

Docker build was not run locally because Docker is not installed in this environment.

Research references used: OpenAI [Chat Completions](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create), OpenAI [Responses](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/create), [links-notation](https://github.com/link-foundation/links-notation), and [lino-objects-codec](https://github.com/link-foundation/lino-objects-codec).

*Monitored by hive-mind with --auto-restart-until-mergeable flag*

Use the most features reach chat UI that supports markdown in both messages and input from https://github.com/link-assistant/react-chat-ui.

Also dialog example should start with Hi/Hello from user. And we should have demo dialog mode, that randomizes in some variations dialogs between user and AI agent, and asks for different languages (also at random) so demo mode is toogle button on top, that shows how dialogs unfolds, waits for 10-20 seconds,  and shows next dialog example, everything should be randomized by having multiple variations in code, that adhere to our original vision.

Also we need to make sure we have all scripts in rust script, that download all datasets from all different sources I mentioned, and all datasets should be saved as Links Notation in ./data folder. Note that our project is Public Domain, so we cannot use exactly the original content (at least for example we change JSON to Links Notation), also for own projects that are in Public Domain we can use whatver we want, but if the content is not yet in Links Notation we should convert it to it.

And use https://github.com/link-foundation/lino-objects-codec (with untyped, indented, the most human readable format).

We need to make sure no `.lino` file is greater than 1500 lines, so it will be always human readable, but files can reference each other if needed.

Reread: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/1

Double check that REQUIREMENTS.md are updated.

And please plan and execute everything in a this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
